MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-12-2021 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 877 6 A2 And Request To Dismiss Cross Actions Pursuant To Cal Rules Of Court Rule 3 1382
Off Calendar Jul-08-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: (Zoom)
Brian Slome, 415.362.2580 for Cross-Defendants Superb Buliders, Inc.
Thomas Chaffee, 415.984.8500 for Defendants Magid Abraham and Linda Abraham.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT NEAL WARD, MAGDALENA VISSER, COMPASS SF I INC., F/K/A PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP, COMPASS CALIFORNIA II INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 877 6 A2 And Request To Dismiss Cross Actions Pursuant To Cal Rules Of Court Rule 3 1382 is Denied. Regardless of whether the total claimed legally allowable damages of plaintiffs Howard and June Park are closer to $6 million or closer to $2 million, cross-complainants Magid and Linda Abraham satisfied their burden of presenting admissible evidence that $100,000 is far too low to be a fair approximation for the proportional responsibility of the Compass cross-defendants and thus is far outside the ballpark. The correspondence between the Abrahams and Compass shows that the Abrahams reasonably relied on Compass to assist the Abrahams with their disclosures and Compass agreed to provide such assistance. The declarations of the Abrahams show that Mr. Ward knew about the Abrahams' recent purchase of the house and their even more recent unsuccessful efforts to sell the house yet Mr. Ward, a self-described elite broker for the sale of high-end houses, did not ask the Abrahams for all the reports relating to the Abrahams' purchase and unsuccessful sale of the house. Nor does Mr. Ward say that he made such a request in his declaration. This failure appears to be a breach of the standard of care, as shown by page 125 of Ms. Hatvany's deposition, and also appears to be causally related to most or all of the Parks' claimed damages. Consequently, the Compass cross-defendants' settlement with the Parks cannot be justified by the no liability position taken in the moving papers or proportionality based on the commission percentage position asserted in the reply papers. The indemnity and general disclosure duties language in the Abrahams/Compass listing agreement does not preclude the Compass cross-defendants' liability for equitable indemnity based on failure of the Compass cross-defendants to satisfy their standard of care. Mr. Slome's request for tweny additional days tp file is granted. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
